title: COVID-19 Genomic Surveillance in Uganda
excerpt: Real-time genomic surveillance of SARS-CoV-2 variants circulating in Uganda to inform public health response and vaccination strategies.
description: This project involves sequencing and analyzing SARS-CoV-2 genomes from clinical samples across Uganda. Using high-performance computing and bioinformatics pipelines, we track viral evolution, identify variants of concern, and assess their potential impact on vaccine effectiveness and disease transmission. Our findings directly inform national COVID-19 response policies.
objectives: Sequence SARS-CoV-2 genomes from diverse regions of Uganda
Identify and track variants of concern
Analyze viral evolution and transmission patterns
Provide data to support public health decision-making

title: Machine Learning for Malaria Diagnosis
excerpt: Developing deep learning models to automatically detect malaria parasites in blood smear images, improving diagnostic accuracy and speed.
description: This project applies computer vision and deep learning to automate malaria diagnosis from microscopy images. We are training convolutional neural networks on thousands of annotated blood smear images to detect and classify Plasmodium parasites. The goal is to develop a tool that can be deployed in resource-limited healthcare settings to improve diagnostic capacity.
objectives: Develop accurate deep learning models for parasite detection
Create a user-friendly diagnostic tool
Validate models in clinical settings
Deploy solution in underserved health facilities

title: Climate Impact on East African Agriculture
excerpt: Using climate models and agricultural data to predict and mitigate the impact of climate change on crop yields in East Africa.
description: This project combines climate science, data analytics, and agricultural expertise to understand how changing climate patterns affect crop productivity in East Africa. We use HPC resources to run complex crop models and analyze decades of climate and yield data. Our findings help farmers and policymakers develop adaptation strategies.
objectives: Model climate change impacts on major crops
Identify vulnerable regions and farming systems
Develop data-driven adaptation strategies
Communicate findings to farmers and policymakers

title: Genomic Analysis of Antimicrobial Resistance
excerpt: Tracking antimicrobial resistance genes in bacterial pathogens through whole-genome sequencing and bioinformatics analysis.
description: Antimicrobial resistance is a growing threat to public health in Africa. This project uses whole-genome sequencing to identify resistance genes in bacterial pathogens isolated from clinical and environmental samples. Our analyses help understand the spread of resistance and inform antimicrobial stewardship programs.
objectives: Sequence genomes of resistant bacterial isolates
Identify and characterize resistance genes
Track transmission of resistant strains
Support antimicrobial stewardship efforts

title: Big Data Analytics for Health Systems
excerpt: Analyzing large-scale health data to identify trends, improve service delivery, and support evidence-based health policy.
description: This project leverages big data analytics to extract insights from health information systems across Uganda. We analyze data on disease patterns, healthcare utilization, and resource allocation to identify gaps and opportunities for improving health service delivery. Our work supports evidence-based decision-making in the health sector.
objectives: Integrate and analyze health system data
Identify disease trends and risk factors
Optimize resource allocation
Support evidence-based health policy

title: Bioinformatics Training Network for Africa
excerpt: Building a pan-African network to strengthen bioinformatics training and capacity across the continent.
description: This collaborative project brings together institutions from across Africa to develop and deliver bioinformatics training programs. We create standardized curricula, organize workshops, and facilitate knowledge exchange to build a strong bioinformatics workforce in Africa.
objectives: Develop standardized bioinformatics curricula
Train instructors across Africa
Organize regional workshops and courses
Create sustainable training network

title: Crop Disease Detection Using Computer Vision
excerpt: Developing mobile applications that use computer vision to identify crop diseases, helping farmers protect their harvests.
description: This project combines machine learning, computer vision, and agriculture to create tools that help farmers identify crop diseases early. Using smartphone cameras, farmers can take pictures of affected plants and receive instant diagnoses and treatment recommendations.
objectives: Collect and annotate crop disease image datasets
Train computer vision models for disease detection
Develop user-friendly mobile application
Pilot with smallholder farmers

title: Population Genomics of African Populations
excerpt: Characterizing genetic diversity in African populations to improve understanding of disease susceptibility and personalized medicine.
description: This project investigates genetic variation in African populations through large-scale genomic studies. Understanding African genetic diversity is crucial for developing effective precision medicine approaches and identifying disease-associated variants relevant to African populations.
objectives: Generate genomic data from diverse African populations
Characterize genetic diversity and population structure
Identify disease-associated genetic variants
Advance precision medicine for African populations
